title: Local Clusters, Cluster Lifecycle, Conformance, Upgrades, Backup, and Production De… · Reconciliation Brief: K8s Version Skew Upgrade Drain
summary: An operations board must distinguish a local minikube proof from production, upgrade a supported cluster without skipped minors, recover control-plane state, and defend remaining risks. Planned artifact: Add and verify the k8s version skew upgrade drain increment in cluster lifecycle and recovery defense. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Plan supported patch and minor upgrades in component order with release notes, deprecated API scans, capacity, drain, disruption, skew, canary, verification, and rollback.
Evaluate Kubernetes conformance, supported versions, distribution lifecycle, cloud integration, add-ons, feature gates, APIs, operational ownership, and vendor support.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.config:
# kubernetes-basics cumulative orchestration portfolio
# Browser labs perform deterministic manifest and evidence review only.
# They never invoke kubectl, read kubeconfig, contact a cluster API, or execute host commands.
workspace: kubernetes
version-baseline:
  kubernetes: 1.36.2
  minikube: 1.38.1
  gateway-api: 1.5.1
  helm: 4.2.2
transfer-boundary: authorized-disposable-cluster
evidence:

steps.title: Predict K8s Version Skew Upgrade Drain · Theory K8s Version Skew Upgrade Drain
steps.instruction:
theory case — A theory board plans a 1.35 to 1.36 control-plane upgrade with skewed kubelets, deprecated APIs, expired recovery assumptions, untested etcd restore, and unclear rollback. Defend compatibility, drain, backup, staged upgrade, recovery, and ownership. Competency probe: Plan supported patch and minor upgrades in component order with release notes, deprecated API scans, capacity, drain, disruption, skew, canary, verification, and rollback.

content.title: Worked case pattern
content.text: A worked example board plans a 1.35 to 1.36 control-plane upgrade with skewed kubelets, deprecated APIs, expired recovery assumptions, untested etcd restore, and unclear rollback. Defend compatibility, drain, backup, staged upgrade, recovery, and ownership. Competency probe: Plan supported patch and minor upgrades in component order with release notes, deprecated API scans, capacity, drain, disruption, skew, canary, verification, and rollback. A defensible solution applies K8s Version Skew Upgrade Drain, preserves the governing invariant or value contract, and connects the observed result to cluster lifecycle and recovery defense.

content.code:
# Evidence: k8s-worked-k8s-cluster-lifecycle-upgrade-recovery-4
  - competency: k8s-worked-k8s-cluster-lifecycle-upgrade-recovery-4
    artifact: cluster-recovery-defense.yaml
    decision: worked k8s cluster lifecycle upgrade recovery 4
    environment: air-gapped release cluster using an internal registry mirror
    fault-injection: drop one metrics series during an autoscaling surge
    retained-proof: accessible incident timeline with causal regression evidence
    case-key: worked4
    change: |
      baseline: kubernetes-1.36.2
      local-proof: minikube-1.38.1
      distribution: supported-and-conformant
      upgrade: patch-current-then-next-minor
      order: api-server-then-controllers-then-kubelet
      drain: capacity-and-pdb-verified
      etcd: encrypted-snapshot-restored
      application-state: independently-restored
      rto-rpo: rehearsed
    verify: kubectl get nodes -o json && kubeadm upgrade plan && etcdctl snapshot status backup.db
    failure: Reject minikube as production proof, skipped minor upgrades, kubelet-first order, drain without capacity, etcd-only recovery, or backup without restore.
    repair: Confirm support and skew, scan removed APIs, canary components and workloads, preserve keys and state, rehearse restore order, and verify user service.
content.caption: Runnable Kubernetes object and transfer-evidence contract example; predict its result before running, then change one boundary input.

content.title: Non-worked case
content.text: Skipping minor versions and upgrading kubelets before API servers is a supported shortcut when workloads still run. This fails the K8s Version Skew Upgrade Drain contract because it omits a precondition, invariant, ownership rule, or counterexample needed by this exact case.
